hawkish_phrases: in view of the expected temporary nature of the shock...it did not seem appropriate to lower the deposit facility rate on this occasion
maintain the option of future cuts in the policy rate in case warranted
cuts in the short-term policy rate were more suited to setting the medium-term orientation of monetary policy than navigating a path through a temporary period of elevated uncertainty
some reservations were also expressed regarding the case for expanding the public sector purchase programme
most members were of the view that increasing the APP would be more effective in lowering risk-free rates
dovish_phrases: a decisive monetary policy response was required in the present circumstances
the spread of the coronavirus was a major shock to the world and the euro area economies, with severe near-term implications
the risks surrounding the euro area growth outlook were clearly on the downside
a substantial recession could be envisaged
stand ready to adjust all of its measures, as appropriate, to ensure that inflation moved towards its aim in a sustained manner
summary: The ECB held rates unchanged but unanimously approved a major easing package including €120 billion in additional asset purchases until year-end, enhanced TLTRO III terms at 25bp below the deposit rate, and bridge LTROs, while explicitly declining to cut the deposit facility rate despite severe coronavirus-related downside risks to growth and inflation.
